evals.prompt: 專案開始時幫我建立系統設計書，並用 Agile 方式保留 IBM SAA 的 good parts
evals.expected_output: The skill classifies whether project-level architecture docs are required, uses SAA good-parts vocabulary, and preserves Agile/YAGNI boundaries.
expectations: The response checks whether .agents/steering/product.md, tech.md, and structure.md already exist, plus same-name HTML or equivalent legacy docs.
The response uses Common User Access, Common Programming Interface, Common Communications Support, and cross-platform consistency as lightweight vocabulary.
The response avoids big-upfront architecture and allows not_yet_required when evidence is thin.

evals.prompt: project review 前檢查 .agents/steering/product|tech|structure markdown/HTML 是否和 specs 一致
evals.expected_output: The skill compares architecture markdown/HTML against accepted spec and review evidence, classifies stale or missing docs, and avoids overclaim.
expectations: The response reads steering markdown/HTML as narrative input, not readiness authority.
The response classifies architecture state as current, needs_update, missing_but_required, not_yet_required, or stale_or_overclaiming.
The response claim-caps project review language against review.md, SPECS.md, RTM.md, and evidence metadata.

evals.prompt: 幫 code-review 先整理這個專案的高階架構資訊和 GraphRAG 查詢方向
evals.expected_output: The skill produces an Architecture Context Packet and focused code-review query suggestions while treating graph output as evidence input only.
expectations: The response includes system boundary, modules/services, bounded contexts, critical data flows, public contracts, trust boundaries, high-blast-radius files, graph query suggestions, open questions, and evidence refs.
The response suggests focused code-review query intents such as architecture, bounded-context, impact, dependency-path, developer-routing, capability-inventory, or search-code graph-only.
The response states that code-review graph output is evidence input, not authority over checked-out code or accepted spec artifacts.

evals.prompt: Sprint review 發現多個 specs 之間的架構 ownership 和 handoff 不一致，請用 agentic scrum governance 和 system-architect 協助判斷下一步
evals.expected_output: The response treats architecture drift as a sprint review/retro signal, delegates architecture doc refresh to system-architect, and preserves readiness authority in review.md.
expectations: The response recognizes architecture drift or ownership ambiguity as a review/retro input.
The response routes architecture markdown/HTML creation or refresh to system-architect.
The response does not treat architecture docs as readiness verdict authority.
The response does not promote the issue to broad skill churn without repetition and owner-resolution evidence.
